prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `3490cc26`.
codex_response:
The rule is a greedy walk over `2x2` blocks: start at the red block, repeatedly connect it to the nearest visible blue block that shares its row or column and has no other block in between, then continue from that newly reached blue block. I implemented that verifier in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3490cc26/verifier.py#L12) and a generator in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3490cc26/generator.py#L186) that explicitly builds sparse orthogonal chains plus optional blue distractors, then rejects any sample whose actual greedy walk differs from the intended family.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint as a starting point and discarded the broader Sonnet summary because it over-described the task as a fuller network closure; that correction is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3490cc26/notes.md#L1). No new reusable primitive was needed; the task-local block extraction and visibility logic lives in [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3490cc26/helpers.py), with package wiring in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_3490cc26/__init__.py).
